summary: Fair‑quality tape of a 1969 show shines on High Time, Lovelight and a wild Me & My Uncle tease, though the first set falters and the recording has some cuts.
review: The second set delivers the highlights – a scorching "High Time", a rocking "Lovelight" and an exciting tease at the end of "Me and My Uncle". The first set is considered weak and the lack of a Dark Star disappoints some listeners, while the tape suffers from occasional cuts and awkward track gaps. Overall the performance is enjoyable but the technical imperfections keep it from being a top‑tier recording.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviews praise several standout songs and the second set, but note first‑set weakness, missing Dark Star and tape cut/track‑gap issues; combined show (70%) and recording (30%) weighting yields a solid above‑average rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Tape has noticeable cuts (e.g., "Too bad for the cut"), silent gaps between tracks, but Pigpen's Fender keys are clear and overall sound is acceptable for a 1969 source.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set praised as strong; first set deemed uninteresting ('Don't bother with the first'). Listeners note the absence of a Dark Star and minor track‑spacing issues between China Cat Sunflower and I Know You Rider.

band_member_comments.Keys: Pigpen's Fender keyboards are highlighted as prominent and enjoyable.
